title: 3D Periodic Table with Discovery Timeline
prompt:
Create an interactive 3D periodic table with a time control slider that filters elements by their year of discovery. Use Three.js for all 3D rendering.

## Scene Setup
- Camera: PerspectiveCamera at position (0, 5, 25), looking at origin, FOV 60.
- Lights: AmbientLight (0xffffff, 0.6) and DirectionalLight (0xffffff, 0.8) at position (10, 15, 10).
- Background: Dark gradient, color 0x0a0a2e.
- OrbitControls enabled for camera rotation/zoom.

## Periodic Table Layout
- Render the first 36 elements (H through Kr) as 3D boxes (BoxGeometry 0.9 x 0.9 x 0.4) arranged in a standard periodic table grid layout.
- Each box face displays: atomic number (top), element symbol (center, large), and element name (bottom) using canvas textures or SpriteMaterial.
- Color-code boxes by element category: alkali metals (0xff6b6b), alkaline earth (0xffa07a), transition metals (0x4ecdc4), metalloids (0x95e1d3), nonmetals (0xa8e6cf), noble gases (0xdda0dd), other metals (0xf7dc6f), halogens (0x87ceeb).
- Position elements in standard periodic table positions: Row 1 has H at col 1 and He at col 18. Row 2: Li(1), Be(2), B(13), C(14), N(15), O(16), F(17), Ne(18). Row 3: Na(1)...Ar(18). Row 4: K(1)...Kr(18) including transition metals in cols 3-12. Use spacing of 1.1 units per column and 1.1 units per row.

## Time Control
- Add an HTML range slider (id='timeSlider') at the bottom of the screen, range 1669 to 1900, default value 1900, step 1.
- Add a label (id='yearLabel') showing 'Year: {value}' above the slider.
- When the slider value changes, elements discovered AFTER the selected year should become semi-transparent (opacity 0.15) and scale down to (0.5, 0.5, 0.5). Elements discovered ON or BEFORE the selected year remain fully opaque (opacity 1.0) and scale (1, 1, 1). Animate the transition with a 0.3s tween.
- Use approximate discovery years: H:1766, He:1868, Li:1817, Be:1798, B:1808, C:-3750, N:1772, O:1774, F:1886, Ne:1898, Na:1807, Mg:1755, Al:1825, Si:1824, P:1669, S:-2000, Cl:1774, Ar:1894, K:1807, Ca:1808, Sc:1879, Ti:1791, V:1801, Cr:1797, Mn:1774, Fe:-5000, Co:1735, Ni:1751, Cu:-9000, Zn:1746, Ga:1875, Ge:1886, As:-300, Se:1817, Br:1826, Kr:1898.

## Element Selection
- On clicking an element box (raycasting), highlight it with a glowing outline (emissive color boost or wireframe overlay) and display an info panel (id='infoPanel') as an HTML overlay showing: Symbol, Name, Atomic Number, Category, Discovery Year.
- Pressing Escape or clicking empty space deselects the element and hides the info panel.

## Category Filter Buttons
- Add a row of small HTML buttons (id='filterButtons' container) at the top: 'All', 'Alkali', 'Alkaline', 'Transition', 'Nonmetal', 'Noble Gas', 'Metalloid', 'Halogen', 'Other Metal'.
- Clicking a category button highlights only elements of that category (others become dim, opacity 0.2). Clicking 'All' restores all elements. The active filter button should have a distinct style (e.g., bright border).

## Keyboard Controls
- Press 'R' to reset camera to initial position with smooth animation.
- Press 'T' to toggle between 3D grid view and a flat 2D layout (all z=0 vs z based on period number).
- Press '+' or '=' to increment the year slider by 10. Press '-' to decrement by 10. Clamp to slider range.

## State Management
Expose window.__3D_STATE__ with:
- `totalElements`: number (should be 36)
- `currentYear`: number (current slider value)
- `visibleCount`: number of elements with opacity === 1.0 at current year
- `selectedElement`: string or null (symbol of selected element, e.g., 'Fe')
- `activeFilter`: string ('All' by default, or category name)
- `cameraPosition`: {x, y, z} current camera position
- `viewMode`: '3D' or '2D'
- `elementsData`: array of objects with {symbol, atomicNumber, discoveryYear, opacity, category}

Update __3D_STATE__ every frame in the animation loop.
physics_constraints: No physics simulation required. Elements must maintain grid positions. Opacity transitions should be smooth. Year filtering must be historically accurate based on provided discovery years. Camera orbit must not allow negative y (floor clamp).
